items.title: Strava declares war on scrapers ahead of IPO
items.summary: Strava will charge a flat monthly fee from developers to access its API

items.title: Unastella, a South Korean rocket startup that launched from home, raises $24M
items.summary: The Seoul-based rocket startup is developing its own launch vehicles and engines.

items.summary: Environmental activist Erin Brockovich has a new mission.

items.title: This weekend’s two biggest movies were both directed by YouTubers
items.summary: The YouTube-to-prestige-horror pipeline is looking very strong.

items.title: Making sense of the debate over AI psychosis
items.summary: On the latest episode of Equity, we debate whether tech CEOs are "uniquely prone to AI psychosis."

items.title: Black founders raise highest amount of quarterly funding since 2022, but there’s a catch
items.summary: Speaking to TechCrunch, Crunchbase’s head of research Gené Teare, said the factors holding back Black founders include “access to networks, relationships, and early introductions."

items.title: What happens in Vega$: steroids, swimmers, and a billion-dollar hustle
items.summary: The Enhanced Games — a singular sporting competition where a majority of the athletes were on performance enhancing drugs — may herald a new business model that the tech industry is ready to embrace.

items.title: SoftBank says it will invest up to €75 billion to build French data centers
items.summary: The goal, the firm said, is to develop and operate up to 5 gigawatts of additional data center capacity.

items.title: Snap alums unveil Ghost Angels fund
items.summary: A group of 20 Snap alumni has come together to launch a fund called Ghost Angels to back the next generation of social media.

items.summary: The golden age of Microsoft's Github Copilot appears to be at an end.

items.summary: Meta seems to be making big bets on AI-powered hardware.

items.title: I put Google’s 24/7 AI assistant Gemini Spark to work, and it’s actually pretty useful
items.summary: Gemini Spark helps automate everyday tasks, from inbox summaries to local event planning, but it’s unclear why Google made it a separate product.

items.title: The groupthink boom: what three top VCs really think about the AI frenzy
items.summary: "If you're 22 years old in San Francisco and building something in AI, there may be a seed term sheet in your inbox — but if you're 19, oh my God, this means you're really good; you might already have a Series A [offer]," said one, half-kiddingly.

items.title: As the browser wars heat up, here are the hottest alternatives to Chrome and Safari in 2026
items.summary: We’ve compiled an overview of some of the top alternative browsers available today aiming to challenge Chrome and Safari.

items.title: This $300 pizza oven can easily help elevate your summer pizza nights
items.summary: The Ninja Artisan Outdoor Pizza Oven is aimed at people who want delicious pizza nights without having to deal with things like propane or wood pellets, unlike many other pizza ovens.

items.title: TikTok’s road to becoming a super app
items.summary: TikTok may be working to become the app that people use for most of their digital activities.

items.title: Founders seize on Indian court ruling to revive criticism of Google’s ad business
items.summary: The ruling drew support from founders, while lawyers said it could force platforms to revisit how they handle trademarked keywords.

items.title: SpaceX awarded $6.45B in Space Force contracts ahead of IPO
items.summary: SpaceX already generated one-fifth of its 2025 revenue from government contracts, the company revealed in its IPO filing.
